new_classes.definition_en: Undenatured ethyl alcohol with an alcoholic strength by volume of less than 80 % vol, and spirits, liqueurs and other spirituous beverages. These are alcoholic liquids generally intended for human consumption, prepared either directly by distillation (with or without added flavouring), or by mixing ethyl alcohol or distilled spirits with other beverages or substances.

A production process by which spirits and other spirituous beverages of heading 2208 are prepared directly by distilling fermented material, with or without added flavouring, yielding an alcoholic liquid with an alcoholic strength by volume of less than 80 % vol.

coverage_explanation: The axioms capture the three principal distinguishing criteria from the legal text: (1) alcoholic strength below 80 % vol (decimalRange restriction on alcoholByVolumePercent), (2) undenatured character (hasValue isDenatured false), and (3) production by distillation (someValuesFrom DistillationProcess). However, the legal text also mentions preparation by mixing ethyl alcohol or distilled spirits with other beverages or substances (a second production route), which cannot be fully expressed without introducing a MixingProcess class and a disjunction over production routes. The disjunction between DistillationProcess and GrapeWineProduction is expressed as a complement restriction to clarify the boundary with heading 2204, but a full coverage of the mixing/blending route and flavouring sub-cases is not captured. Coverage is therefore set to 0.75.
